subject: Statistics
title: Publication Bias in Medicine
description: Learn Publication Bias in Medicine in one minute.

hook.text: Antidepressant trials published in journals show 94% positive results. FDA records show only 51% positive.

buildup.text: Positive results get published. Negative results sit in drawers. This is the file drawer problem.

discovery.text: Doctors prescribe based on published evidence. If failures are hidden, the evidence looks better than reality.

twist.text: Trial registries now require reporting all results. But compliance is poor and enforcement is weak.

climax.text: Publication bias distorts the entire evidence base. It makes ineffective treatments look effective.

punchline.text: What doesn't get published matters as much as what does.

quiz.question: What is publication bias?
options: Positive results are more likely to be published than negative ones, distorting the evidence
